text: Plumb must be installable via both pip and uv using the package name 'plumb-dev' on PyPI
The CLI command must be 'plumb'
The package must include a comprehensive project description sourced from the README.md file
Tests must support comment-based markers using '# plumb:req-XXXXXXXX' format for linking to requirements
Tests must support function name-based linking using 'test_req_XXXXXXXX_' format
Both linking formats must be supported for backwards compatibility
The system must filter for spec-relevant content during decision extraction in both conversation analysis and diff analysis methods
The system must implement semantic similarity checking to handle duplicate or similar decisions effectively
Classification tasks must not generate reasoning traces to maintain optimal performance
LLM-based deduplication must utilize Claude Haiku 4.5 as the designated model
The deduplicate_decisions() function must accept a use_llm parameter that defaults to False
Plumb must use the Anthropic Claude SDK via the user's existing account as its inference provider
Claude Sonnet 4.6 must serve as the default model for all programs
All LLM-powered functions must be implemented as DSPy programs, not open-ended agents
Plumb must operate as a git hook and CLI tool without changing how the user writes code
The pre-commit hook must ensure that every committed state has been reviewed and approved
A commit must represent a fully reconciled snapshot of spec, tests, and code
Conversation analysis must be opportunistic, using Claude Code session data when available
When Claude Code session data is not available, Plumb must fall back to diff-only analysis
The system must prevent duplicate decisions through a multi-stage pipeline using exact matching, Jaccard similarity, and LLM semantic analysis
Only prescriptive choices that affect system design, behavior, architecture, or data models must be captured as decisions
Process observations, tooling choices, and diagnostic findings must be filtered out
Spec updates must use a search-and-replace approach with edits containing old_text/new_text pairs
Section updates must use exact header matching first, then fall back to normalized matching
Normalized matching must handle stripped whitespace and case-insensitive comparisons
Plumb must support loading environment variables from .env files to manage configuration settings
The 'plumb init' command must create a .env file in the project directory
The system must support ANTHROPIC_API_KEY configuration through both environment variables and .env files
Tests must be linked to requirements through requirement ID comments to establish traceability
All tests must be organized in the tests/ directory with specific test files for each component
Tests without requirement links must be treated as sync violations
The system must support file ignore patterns through a '.plumbignore' file
The system must provide a '--all' command option for the 'plumb approve' command to approve all changes at once
Generated cache and coverage files must be excluded from commits
The system must include a 'check' command as an alias for manual decision scanning
Plumb must handle conversations that span multiple Claude Code session files by reading and merging them chronologically
The sync command must provide progress indicators with status updates
The workflow must require an explicit sync step after approving decisions
Sync output must be staged before re-committing changes
The system must use a branch-sharded structure for decision logs
The system must provide CLI commands for merging decision logs from different branches
Users must be able to migrate existing decision logs from the old monolithic format to the new branch-sharded layout
The 'plumb merge-decisions <branch>' command must allow users to merge decisions from feature branches back to main
A find_decision_branch() function must help locate which branch file contains a specific decision ID
The WholeFileSpecUpdater must take full spec content and decisions as input
The WholeFileSpecUpdater must output section_updates for existing sections and new_sections for brand new sections
The system must accept rewriting whole sections instead of making surgical edits for markdown specs
An OutlineMerger component must handle structural changes when new sections need to be added or content needs reordering
The system must include helper functions _clean_duckdb_row() and _to_python_native() for DuckDB result type conversion
Helper functions must convert DuckDB result types to Python native types for Pydantic compatibility
Comprehensive test coverage must be implemented for migration and merge functionality
Test coverage must include edge cases and error conditions
DSPy programs must support program-specific model configuration through a model configuration array in config.json
Pattern parsing logic must be modularized into separate functions for reusability across programs
The system must include an extract_outline function that extracts markdown headers from content
The programs module must include token estimation, chunking, and concurrent mapper functionality
The system must handle large datasets by breaking them into token-budget-sized chunks
The system must use chunked mapping with ThreadPoolExecutor for concurrent processing
The system must support different merge strategies for result combination
The system must track which requirements have been modified and only send dirty requirements to the mapper
Plumb must store all state in a '.plumb/' folder at the root of the user's repository
The '.plumb/' folder must be committed to version control
Plumb must intercept commits via a git pre-commit hook
The commit must be the gate, and nothing must be committed until decisions have been reviewed
The hook must validate API access before proceeding with analysis
If API authentication fails, the hook must exit non-zero and block the commit
The hook must analyze the staged diff and the Claude Code conversation log using the unified conversation reading system
The system must auto-detect Claude Code sessions vs legacy logs
The system must read from Claude Code's actual session files at ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-path>/<uuid>.jsonl
The hook must write pending decisions to branch-specific decision log files
The system must use filesystem-safe path sanitization
The system must set the last_extracted_at timestamp
The hook must print a machine-readable JSON summary of pending decisions to stdout and exit non-zero when there are pending decisions
Claude Code's skill must read the hook output and begin presenting decisions to the user using AskUserQuestion format
Decisions must be presented one at a time
The system must include explicit safeguards to never approve, reject, or edit decisions on the user's behalf
Users must be able to approve multiple pending decisions efficiently using the --all flag
Decision operations must support branch-specific handling through branch parameter support
For rejected decisions, the skill must invoke 'plumb modify <id>'
The modify command must modify the staged code, run tests, and report the result conversationally
The skill must re-run 'git commit' once all decisions are resolved
The commit must only land when there are zero pending decisions
The system must draft commit messages after decision review and include the list of approved decisions
When API authentication fails, the system must raise a custom PlumbAuthError exception
The PlumbAuthError must alert the user and provide clear instructions to set their API key
API key validation must be implemented as a separate validate_api_access function
The validate_api_access function must be called before all LLM operations
All authentication functionality must have comprehensive test coverage
Existing tests must be updated to mock API validation calls
The system must support environment variable management using .env files with python-dotenv dependency
The system must use gitignore-style patterns for ignore functionality
Default patterns must be used when no .plumbignore file exists
After commit completion, the post-commit hook must clear the last_extracted_at timestamp
The conversation parser must handle Claude Code's type/message schema format
Tool usage blocks must be converted to text format '[tool: Name] description' for consistency
The system must include comprehensive documentation for the Plumb skill
Documentation must include detailed instructions for managing spec/test/code alignment and decision review processes
The system must implement intelligent deduplication by first running Jaccard similarity filtering
LLM deduplication must be applied as a second pass when 2 or more candidates remain
The system must use DSPy's context manager with Haiku LM for deduplication while preserving Sonnet for other operations
The deduplication process must prioritize approved and synced decisions over recent ones in the comparison set
The system must use an expanded context window of 200 decisions instead of 50 for more comprehensive duplicate detection
The system must generate complete and runnable tests rather than stub files with TODO comments
Test generation must use increased context limits to accommodate complete test code
The test generator must produce fully functional test code that can be executed immediately
Test generation must only run if tests don't already exist
The system must provide read_all_decisions() as the primary API function for accessing decisions across all branches
The review command must pre-compute decision branches to optimize performance
The review command must ensure correct shard targeting for updates
Users must be able to migrate from the monolithic decision file to the sharded layout using the plumb migrate command
The migrate command must convert existing decisions.jsonl to decisions/main.jsonl
The system must implement spec updates using a single LLM call per spec file
The output schema must contain section_updates and new_sections
A second call must be made only when new_sections is non-empty
Structural reasoning for section placement must be kept separate from content generation
Edit operations must be kept simple without complex operation sets
The system must accept the risk of unintended edits in exchange for performance gains while mitigating through clear prompting
The legacy decisions path function must be maintained for migration detection purposes
The primary system must operate on branch-specific decision logs
The hook must analyze the staged diff when committing from the terminal
The system must read Claude Code's native session files directly from auto-detected paths
The system must extract prescriptive choices while excluding observations and diagnostics
Within-batch similarity deduplication must prevent duplicate decisions in the same batch
Time awareness must use both last_commit datetime and last_extracted_at timestamp as cutoffs
The system must take the later of the two timestamps to determine which conversations to process
The hook must write pending decisions to decisions.jsonl
Current decisions and recent decisions from the last couple commits must be passed to the deduplication pass
The hook must print a human-readable summary of pending decisions and exit non-zero when there are pending decisions
Users must run 'plumb review' in their terminal for interactive decision presentation
The review command must accept keypresses for decision handling
Rejected decisions must be modifiable via 'plumb modify <id>'
The modify command must stage the modified code and report test results
Users must re-run 'git commit' after resolving decisions
Both paths must use the same hook, decision log, per-decision commands, and sync logic
The only difference must be who drives the review loop: Claude Code's skill or the interactive 'plumb review' CLI
The system must use exact deduplication and LLM-based semantic deduplication
groq/openai/gpt-oss-120b must be configured for both decision_deduplicator and question_synthesizer with 8192 max_tokens
Source summaries must be structured as per-file mappings to enable granular tracking
All documentation files including SKILL files and CLAUDE.md must be kept consistent with the same sync workflow requirements
'plumb init' must check that the current directory is a git repository and exit with an error if not
'plumb init' must create the '.plumb/' directory if it does not exist
'plumb init' must prompt the user interactively to provide a path to a spec file or directory of spec markdown files
'plumb init' must validate that the spec path exists and contains .md files
'plumb init' must use recursive search to find markdown files within directories
'plumb init' must suggest discovered spec files
'plumb init' must prompt the user to provide a path to a test file or test directory
'plumb init' must validate that the test path exists
'plumb init' must scan repository for test directories and files to provide suggestions
'plumb init' must write '.plumb/config.json' with the provided paths
'plumb init' must create a '.plumbignore' file in the project root if it does not exist
'plumb init' must install the git pre-commit hook by writing a script to '.git/hooks/pre-commit'
The pre-commit hook script must call 'plumb hook'
The pre-commit hook script must be set as executable
'plumb init' must install the Claude Code skill locally by copying 'plumb/skill/SKILL.md' to '.claude/skills/plumb/SKILL.md' in the project root
'plumb init' must create '.claude/skills/plumb/' directories if they do not exist
The skill installation must be project-local only
Plumb must never write to the user's global '~/.claude/' directory
'plumb init' must append a Plumb status block to 'CLAUDE.md' at the project root
'plumb init' must create 'CLAUDE.md' if it does not exist
'plumb init' must run 'plumb parse-spec' to do an initial parse of the spec into requirements
'plumb init' must print a confirmation summary to the terminal
The confirmation must include confirmation that the skill was installed at '.claude/skills/plumb/SKILL.md'
'plumb hook' must be called automatically by the git pre-commit hook
'plumb hook' must not be intended to be called directly by users but must work if called manually
'plumb hook' must read '.plumb/config.json' and exit 0 silently if not found
'plumb hook' must get the current staged diff via 'git diff --cached'
'plumb hook' must get the current branch name
'plumb hook' must detect amends by comparing the HEAD commit's parent SHA to last_commit
If HEAD's parent SHA equals last_commit, the hook must delete decisions where commit_sha equals last_commit before re-running analysis
'plumb hook' must detect broken references by checking all SHAs in decisions.jsonl against git history
Unreachable SHAs must be flagged with 'ref_status': 'broken'
A warning must be included in output for broken references
'plumb hook' must run the Diff Analysis DSPy program on the staged diff
'plumb hook' must attempt to locate and read the Claude Code conversation log
If conversation log is found, the hook must read and chunk turns since last_commit timestamp
The hook must use unified conversation reading interface to support multiple sessions
The hook must merge conversation turns from all relevant session files modified after the last commit
Conversation turns must be sorted chronologically
The hook must handle multi-line assistant responses that span multiple JSONL lines
tool_use blocks must be converted to '[tool: Name] description' format in conversation turns
The hook must run Decision Extraction per chunk
If conversation log is not found, the hook must skip conversation analysis and note 'conversation_available': false
The hook must filter out decisions marked as non-spec-relevant during extraction before creating Decision records
The hook must merge and deduplicate decisions across chunks using DuckDB for cross-shard queries
Deduplication must check against all existing decisions, both pending and resolved
The hook must implement within-batch similarity deduplication to compare new decisions against each other using Jaccard similarity check
For each decision with no associated question, the hook must run Question Synthesizer with program-specific model configuration
The hook must write all new decisions with status: 'pending' to decisions.jsonl
The hook must implement decisions sharding using a TDD approach with core refactor, DuckDB integration, caller updates, new commands, and comprehensive testing coverage
The hook must run 'plumb parse-spec' to update requirements cache for any modified spec files
The hook must batch spec updates across all decisions instead of running spec update for each individual decision
The hook must use search-and-replace pairs pattern for spec updates
LLM must return list of {old_text, new_text} operations instead of rewriting entire sections
The hook must implement a two-tier approach for spec updates: Tier 1 for existing section updates and Tier 2 for structural changes
For structural changes, the hook must use outline-first approach where LLM returns updated header outline first
The hook must fill in content for new sections after getting the outline
The hook must create OutlineMerger signature for conditional second call
OutlineMerger must take current outline and new headers and return merged outline with proper positioning
If pending decisions exist, the hook must check whether it is running in a TTY or as a subprocess
In TTY mode, the hook must print a human-readable summary of pending decisions with instructions to run 'plumb review'
In non-TTY mode, the hook must print a machine-readable JSON object to stdout with specific schema
The hook must exit non-zero in both TTY and non-TTY cases when pending decisions exist
If no pending decisions exist, the hook must run 'plumb coverage'
The hook must print a brief summary when no pending decisions exist
The hook must update last_commit and last_commit_branch in config.json
The hook must exit 0 when no pending decisions exist, allowing the commit to proceed
The hook must never exit non-zero due to an internal Plumb error
If Plumb itself fails, it must print a warning to stderr and exit 0 so the commit is not blocked
'plumb hook --dry-run' must run the full hook analysis on staged changes but not write to decisions.jsonl
'plumb hook --dry-run' must always exit 0
'plumb hook --dry-run' must be equivalent to 'plumb diff'
'plumb diff' must preview what Plumb will capture from currently staged changes in read-only mode
'plumb diff' must read staged changes via 'git diff --cached'
'plumb diff' must run Diff Analysis on the staged diff
'plumb diff' must read and chunk the conversation log if available
'plumb diff' must run Decision Extraction per chunk
'plumb diff' must print a preview including summary of staged code changes, estimated number of decisions, and brief description of each decision
'plumb diff' must make no writes to '.plumb/'
'plumb review' must provide interactive review of pending decisions for terminal use
'plumb review' must read '.plumb/decisions.jsonl' and filter for status == 'pending'
'plumb review' must accept an optional '--branch <name>' flag to filter by branch
When multiple session files exist, 'plumb review' must use the most recent session file by modification time
'plumb review' must match the current session ID for session selection
If no pending decisions exist, 'plumb review' must print 'No pending decisions.' and exit 0
For each pending decision, 'plumb review' must display the framing question, decision made, branch, file and line references, ref_status, and related spec text
'plumb review' must prompt the user with options: [a]pprove, [r]eject, [e]dit, [s]kip
For reject option, 'plumb review' must prompt for reason and optionally run 'plumb modify <id>' automatically
For edit option, 'plumb review' must allow user to provide replacement decision text
After all decisions are resolved, 'plumb review' must run 'plumb sync' for all approved/edited decisions
Decision classification must default spec_relevant field to True when LLM classification is uncertain
'plumb approve <id>' must approve a single decision by ID and update its status to 'approved'
'plumb approve <id>' must run 'plumb sync' for that decision only
The approve command status display must include a visual bar chart representation for coverage statistics
The approve command must use Rich's Status spinner that updates in-place for progress indication
'plumb reject <id>' must reject a single decision by ID and update its status to 'rejected'
'plumb reject <id>' must record the rejection reason
'plumb reject <id>' must not modify code or spec
'plumb edit <id> "<new decision text>"' must replace the decision text with user-provided text
'plumb edit <id>' must update status to 'edited'
'plumb edit <id>' must run 'plumb sync' for that decision only
'plumb modify <id>' must modify staged code to satisfy a rejected decision
'plumb modify <id>' must read the decision object and verify status == 'rejected'
'plumb modify <id>' must read the staged diff
'plumb modify <id>' must call the Claude API with staged diff, decision, rejection reason, current spec, and modification instruction
'plumb modify <id>' must apply the proposed modification to the staged files
'plumb modify <id>' must run pytest to verify tests still pass after modification
If tests pass, 'plumb modify <id>' must print the resulting diff, stage the modified files, and update decision status to 'rejected_modified'
If tests fail, 'plumb modify <id>' must print the failure output, not stage the modification, prompt user to resolve manually, and update status to 'rejected_manual'
In non-TTY mode, 'plumb modify <id>' must return a machine-readable JSON result
plumb must never commit the modification, only stage it
'plumb sync' must update the spec and tests to reflect all approved and edited decisions
'plumb sync' must add early exit check to avoid expensive operations when no unsynced decisions exist
'plumb sync' must read decisions with status 'approved' or 'edited' that have not yet been synced
For each spec file, 'plumb sync' must run WholeFileSpecUpdater to process all decisions affecting the spec file together
WholeFileSpecUpdater must return section_updates for existing sections and new_sections for brand new sections
If new_sections is non-empty, 'plumb sync' must run OutlineMerger to determine proper positioning
'plumb sync' must write the updated spec file to disk using temp file → rename
'plumb sync' must fix newline formatting by ensuring proper newlines between section headers and body content
'plumb sync' must run Test Generator to generate organized tests for requirements not covered by existing tests
Tests must be organized by functionality with proper requirement links in the format 'test_<req_id>_<description>'
The test generator must read only source files referenced by file_refs on synced decisions for context
'plumb sync' must write generated tests to 'test_generated.py' using temp file → rename
'plumb sync' must run 'plumb parse-spec' to re-cache requirements
'plumb sync' must set synced_at on each processed decision
'plumb sync' must print a summary of spec sections updated and test files created
The system must use '.plumbignore' file with default patterns for documentation, build files, and IDE configurations
'plumb parse-spec' must parse all spec markdown files into an explicit list of requirements and cache them
'plumb parse-spec' must read all markdown files in spec_paths from config.json
For each file or paragraph block, 'plumb parse-spec' must run Requirement Parser to produce explicit, testable requirement statements
'plumb parse-spec' must assign each requirement a stable ID based on a hash of its content
'plumb parse-spec' must write results to '.plumb/requirements.json'
Requirements with matching hashes must not be re-processed, preserving existing metadata
'plumb parse-spec' must use regex patterns PLUMB_MARKER_RE and FUNC_NAME_RE to extract requirement IDs from test files
'plumb parse-spec' must process all requirements and AST summaries of every non-test .py file through CodeCoverageMapper using chunked mapping
The system must use fan-out chunking to group source files under token budget with default 60000 tokens
Requirements must be broadcasted to each chunk
The system must implement granular per-file hashing with structured v2 cache format
The v2 cache format must track individual requirement results for efficient cache invalidation
Existing incremental caching logic must be preserved by applying chunking inside the LLM call after dirty requirements are determined
Source file paths must be extracted from evidence strings using pattern matching against known files
The system must use greedy packing to group source/test summaries into chunks
The system must use ThreadPoolExecutor for concurrent chunk processing
Results from multiple chunks must be merged using custom logic that ORs 'implemented' flags and concatenates evidence strings per requirement_id
'plumb coverage' must report coverage across all three dimensions
For code coverage, 'plumb coverage' must run 'pytest --cov' and parse output to report line coverage percentage
For spec-to-test coverage, 'plumb coverage' must check whether tests reference or map to each requirement
The system must use string-matching to look for requirement IDs literally in test files
The system must scan for link markers in test files to determine coverage
'plumb coverage' must report count and percentage covered for spec-to-test coverage
For spec-to-code coverage, 'plumb coverage' must use the requirements cache to check whether each requirement has corresponding implementation
The system must use string-matching to look for requirement IDs literally in source files
'plumb coverage' must report gaps in spec-to-code coverage
'plumb coverage' must print a formatted table using rich
'plumb coverage' must use LLM calls to refresh the requirements cache for accurate coverage analysis
'plumb coverage' must measure and report coverage improvement achieved by marker injection

'plumb status' must print a human-readable summary including tracked spec files, total requirements, number of tests, pending decisions with branch breakdown, decisions with broken git references, last sync commit, and coverage summary
Plumb must ship with a Claude Code skill file at 'plumb/skill/SKILL.md'
During 'plumb init', the skill file must be copied to '.claude/SKILL.md' in the project root as a project-local installation only
The skill must never be installed globally
Claude Code must automatically read files in '.claude/' at the start of each session
No additional configuration must be required after 'plumb init'
The skill file must provide structured guidance for AI-assisted development workflow
The skill must teach Claude Code the Plumb workflow so it can guide the user naturally through the development process
The skill must provide the machine-readable protocol for parsing hook output and calling per-decision commands during conversational review
The system must support reading Claude Code native session files with JSONL parsing from the ~/.claude/projects/ directory structure
Conversation logs must be located in the real session JSONL files using the format ~/.claude/projects/<project>/<session-uuid>.jsonl
The '.claude/' directory and 'SKILL.md' must be committed to version control
All contributors to the project must get the skill automatically
'plumb init' must append a Plumb status block to 'CLAUDE.md' delimited by comment markers
Future Plumb commands must be able to update the CLAUDE.md block without affecting surrounding content
claude_log_path must be configurable in '.plumb/config.json'
If claude_log_path is not set, Plumb must attempt to auto-detect using common Claude Code log locations
If log is not found, Plumb must skip conversation analysis, note 'conversation_available': false, print a warning, and continue with diff-only analysis
The log must be a JSONL file where each line is a turn with at minimum role, content, and timestamp
Session files must be pre-filtered by modification time to avoid reading old files
Individual entries must be filtered by timestamp
Plumb must read only turns recorded after the last_commit timestamp in config.json
The conversation parser must handle the native session JSONL format with type/message schema used by Claude Code
Chunking must be performed in conversation.py before any DSPy program is called
No LLM must be involved in the chunking step
Chunks must be created deterministically
The primary unit must be user turn: one user message plus all following assistant turns up to but not including the next user message
If a chunk exceeds 6,000 tokens, it must be split at tool call boundaries
If no tool call boundary exists, the chunk must be split at the midpoint of the largest assistant turn
The final assistant turn of the previous chunk must be prepended as a header to the next chunk for overlap
One turn of overlap must preserve continuity without meaningfully increasing size
Before chunking, tool result turns longer than 500 tokens that appear to be raw file reads must be replaced with '[file read: <filename>]'
The heuristic for file reads must be content that begins with a file path or code fence
Assistant messages that span multiple JSONL entries must be properly parsed and reassembled
DecisionExtractor must be called once per chunk with diff_summary passed identically to every call
Results must be merged after all chunks with near-duplicate decisions collapsed into one
The earliest chunk_index must be preserved when collapsing duplicates
On amends, the pre-commit hook must compare HEAD's parent SHA to last_commit
If HEAD's parent SHA equals last_commit, decisions where commit_sha == last_commit must be deleted before re-running analysis
On every hook run, all stored SHAs must be checked against git history
Unreachable SHAs must be flagged 'ref_status': 'broken'
Plumb must not attempt to re-map decisions to new SHAs
Users must review and re-resolve broken-reference decisions manually
The decision log must be append-only with existing lines never modified in place
Status updates must be written as new lines with the same id
The latest line for a given id must be canonical
DiffAnalyzer must take raw unified diff string as input
DiffAnalyzer must output list of change summaries with files_changed, summary, and change_type
DiffAnalyzer must group related changes into logical units and not invent meaning
DecisionExtractor must take chunk and diff_summary as input
DecisionExtractor must output list of decision objects with question, decision, made_by, related_diff_summary, and confidence
DecisionExtractor must extract explicit and implicit decisions but not trivial decisions
QuestionSynthesizer must take a decision object with no associated question as input
QuestionSynthesizer must output a plain-English question framing the decision for a developer
RequirementParser must take markdown string as input
RequirementParser must output list of requirement objects with text and ambiguous fields
RequirementParser must use atomic statements, active voice, no duplicates
Vague statements must be flagged 'ambiguous: true' and excluded unless user approves
SpecUpdater must take spec_section and decision as input
SpecUpdater must output updated markdown for that section
SpecUpdater must capture result of decision as natural requirement with no reference to the decision itself and preserve existing formatting
TestGenerator must take requirements, existing_tests, and code_context as input
TestGenerator must output runnable pytest test code as a Python string
TestGenerator must create one function per requirement with descriptive names, real assertions against actual code, no pytest.skip() or TODO comments, and no overwriting existing tests
CodeModifier must use the Claude API directly, not DSPy
CodeModifier must take staged diff, rejected decision, rejection reason, and current spec as input
CodeModifier must output modified file contents that satisfy the rejection while remaining consistent with the spec
All CLI commands must fail gracefully with clear error messages if config.json is missing or malformed
All DSPy programs must retry on LLM failure with max 2 retries then raise PlumbInferenceError
The git hook must never exit non-zero due to an internal Plumb error
Internal Plumb failures must print a warning to stderr and exit 0
File writes must use temp file → rename to avoid partial writes
If conversation log is unavailable, this must not be considered an error
If 'plumb modify' test run fails, Plumb must not stage the modification and must update decision status to 'rejected_manual'
'plumb status' must operate in cache-only mode to avoid making LLM calls
'plumb coverage' must display progress using an in-place updating spinner, then print the final results table
Session file reading must be optimized by pre-filtering based on file modification times
Plumb itself must be tested with pytest with 80% coverage minimum for v0.1.0
cli.py tests must verify all commands run without error given valid inputs and per-decision commands update decisions.jsonl correctly
Coverage command tests must verify progress feedback with progress bar and status text
Spec and test suggestion function tests must cover various directory scenarios
decision_log.py tests must verify read/write/filter/dedup on .jsonl and latest-line-wins logic for status updates
git_hook.py tests must verify hook produces correct pending decisions given mock diffs and conversation logs, amend detection, and TTY vs non-TTY output formats
conversation.py tests must verify correct chunk boundaries, overlap, noise reduction, metadata, and oversized chunks split at tool call boundaries
programs/ tests must verify each DSPy program produces correctly structured output given fixture inputs for schema validity
programs/ tests must include comprehensive test suite for chunking functionality covering token estimation, item chunking, and chunked mapper execution scenarios
coverage_reporter.py tests must verify correct calculations given mock pytest output

sync.py tests must verify spec and test files updated correctly given approved decisions, no partial writes, and newline formatting for both apply_section_updates() and insert_new_sections() functions
